candidates.observed_problem: Long work loses its main line when a checklist lacks current facts, dependency gates, evidence slots, scope boundaries, and a completion audit.
candidates.repeated_pattern: The owner repeatedly requested CDS-style authoritative Goal documents and rejected loose task lists.
candidates.proposed_rule: Formal long-running Goals must use one authoritative document with facts, scope and non-scope, dependency-ordered phases, independently verifiable nodes, evidence slots, stop conditions, delivery boundaries, and completion audit.
candidates.counterexample_or_limit: A trivial one-command task does not need a large document, but still needs an acceptance signal and truthful evidence.
candidates.verification: Goal lint rejects missing core sections and accepts the repository CDS examples.

candidates.observed_problem: Status prose and visual confidence can drift from the actual command, browser, data, or Git state.
candidates.repeated_pattern: Both projects use explicit tests and status evidence; gongtu repeatedly corrected claims made before source-page or full-path verification.
candidates.proposed_rule: Completion is derived from direct evidence at the same scope as the claim; stale status entries must be marked superseded rather than silently coexisting as current truth.
candidates.counterexample_or_limit: A narrow unit test can prove a helper, but cannot prove a full user path or production readiness.
candidates.verification: Goal state counts only verified tasks and completion audit maps each requirement to evidence.

candidates.observed_problem: Scanning every local repository mixes personal projects, third-party clones, dependencies, and unrelated organizations.
candidates.repeated_pattern: The owner explicitly defined GitHub ownership as a filter for local project scope, not permission to read remote contents.
candidates.proposed_rule: Multi-project discovery must filter local repositories by parsed origin owner and remain read-only unless a project is separately selected for changes.
candidates.counterexample_or_limit: Repositories without a matching origin are excluded even if their directory name looks related; aliases require explicit owner configuration.
candidates.verification: Temporary repositories and the real local roots return only xixinikl-owned origins.

candidates.observed_problem: OCR and derived answers introduced silent corruption and repeated repair work.
candidates.repeated_pattern: The owner repeatedly required OCR only for location, visual source confirmation for facts, original images for diagrams, and complete field-level verification before moving on.
candidates.proposed_rule: High-stakes extraction pipelines must declare a source hierarchy, keep derived data quarantined, preserve source evidence, and gate publication on field-level verification; question media and explanation media are separate contracts.
candidates.counterexample_or_limit: This is not required for ordinary deterministic text files with lossless structured parsers.
candidates.verification: A reusable checklist requires source, locator, field verification, media role, and publish gate.

candidates.observed_problem: Repeatedly adding controls or CSS to an unclear product model made interfaces denser without making the next action clearer.
candidates.repeated_pattern: CanvasStorm moved to MVP-first semantics; gongtu repeatedly separated learning, practice, review, and AI contexts instead of stacking every function on one screen.
candidates.proposed_rule: When users cannot explain the next action or two controls have overlapping semantics, freeze the product/state contract before further visual patching and verify one primary path per state.
candidates.counterexample_or_limit: A local spacing or contrast defect with a stable interaction contract does not require information-architecture redesign.
candidates.verification: Design reviews identify state, primary action, transition, and deferred actions before CSS implementation.

candidates.observed_problem: Chat summaries and old absolute paths become stale, while parallel threads can overwrite or misstate work.
candidates.repeated_pattern: Both projects rely on repository handoffs that name branch, task, changed files, verified and unverified work, next action, and risks.
candidates.proposed_rule: A handoff is a repository-backed fact contract, not a narrative recap; it must identify superseded facts and distinguish implemented, verified, and planned states.
candidates.counterexample_or_limit: A short stateless question needs no formal handoff.
candidates.verification: Profile handoff template includes authoritative source, superseded facts, and explicit unverified items.

candidates.observed_problem: A CI assertion required at least one repair task, so the fully repaired zero-queue state failed.
candidates.repeated_pattern: The defect appeared at the transition from partial completion to full completion and required a zero-state boundary fix.
candidates.proposed_rule: Workflow checks must test empty, partial, and complete states; absence of work is valid when counts and invariants agree.
candidates.counterexample_or_limit: An empty result remains an error when the contract requires at least one configured input.
candidates.verification: System tests include zero-task Goal or learning portfolios where applicable.
